name: Walsh et al. SJT Synthetic Demographic Fairness Pilot Extract
population_or_context: Open-response situational judgment test responses from Casper, an admissions assessment context.
sample_size: 100 original responses and 100 analogous synthetic responses for the fairness pilot; broader automated-scoring experiments used larger Casper response samples.
methods: Extracted the paper's LLM feature extraction, traditional ML scoring, human-score comparison, and synthetic demographic-response fairness pilot.
education_context: Open-response situational judgment test for academic admissions; responses reflect personal and professional attributes rather than classroom correctness tasks.
response_origin: Real Casper responses are used for scoring-system development and evaluation; analogous synthetic responses are created by injecting demographic information into 100 real-response analogs for a fairness pilot.
rubric_label_space: Casper open-response scoring on a 1-9 scale, with LLM-derived features mapped to human-rated constructs.
label_source: Human evaluator ratings provide the real-response ground truth; the synthetic fairness comparison uses model score deltas rather than newly human-adjudicated synthetic correctness labels.
generation_model: Not specified for the demographic-injected synthetic analogs.
generation_method: Analogous synthetic responses with injected demographic information were constructed from 100 original responses for an early fairness experiment.
prompt_strategy: Not reported for synthetic analog generation in the extracted fairness pilot.
evaluation_method: Compared automated LLM evaluations of original responses against analogous synthetic demographic-injected responses; also assessed automated scoring against human ratings.
scorer_use: Fairness stress testing and automated scorer validation for LLM-assisted open-response scoring.
real_response_comparator: Direct paired comparison between 100 original Casper responses and 100 analogous synthetic demographic-injected responses.
reported_metrics: Fairness pilot reports average score deltas near zero for gender and race comparisons; broader scoring sections report agreement and prediction metrics against human evaluations.
limitations: The fairness experiment is described as an early pilot with only 100 original and 100 synthetic responses.
The authors note that small samples prevent reliable between-group effect-size estimates.
Casper SJT admissions responses are not equivalent to ordinary classroom student answers or rubric correctness labels.
